hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: collect
commands.description: Monitor logs and metrics in a .NET application send the results to a chosen destination.

options.name: --urls
options.description: Bindings for the REST api. [default: System.String[]]

options.name: --metricUrls
options.description: Bindings for metrics api. [default: System.String[]]

options.name: --metrics
options.description: Enable publishing of metrics [default: True]

options.name: --diagnostic-port
options.description: The fully qualified path and filename of the diagnostic port to which runtime instances can connect.

options.name: --no-auth
options.description: Turn off authentication. [default: False]

options.name: --temp-apikey
options.description: Generates a new MonitorApiKey for each launch of the process. [default: False]

options.name: --no-http-egress
options.description: Turn off HTTP response egress [default: False]

options.name: --configuration-file-path
options.description: The fully qualified path and filename of the json configuration file you'd like to add to the list of configuration sources.

options.name: --exit-on-stdin-disconnect
options.description: If specified, dotnet-monitor will monitor `stdin`, and if it closes, will exit. This is useful when dotnet-monitor is started remotely without a pseudo-terminal, and the caller wishes for it to exit when the connection is dropped (example: `docker exec -i ...`). [default: False]

commands.name: show
commands.description: Shows configuration, as if dotnet-monitor collect was executed with these parameters.

options.name: --urls
options.description: Bindings for the REST api. [default: System.String[]]

options.name: --metricUrls
options.description: Bindings for metrics api. [default: System.String[]]

options.name: --metrics
options.description: Enable publishing of metrics [default: True]

options.name: --diagnostic-port
options.description: The fully qualified path and filename of the diagnostic port to which runtime instances can connect.

options.name: --no-auth
options.description: Turn off authentication. [default: False]

options.name: --temp-apikey
options.description: Generates a new MonitorApiKey for each launch of the process. [default: False]

options.name: --no-http-egress
options.description: Turn off HTTP response egress [default: False]

options.name: --configuration-file-path
options.description: The fully qualified path and filename of the json configuration file you'd like to add to the list of configuration sources.

options.name: --level
options.description: Configuration level. Unredacted configuration can show sensitive information. [default: Redacted]

options.name: --show-sources
options.description: Show loaded configuration providers from high to low priority [default: False]

commands.name: generatekey
commands.description: Generate api key and hash for authentication.

options.name: --output
options.description: The output format of the API Key configuration. [default: Json]

options.name: --expiration
options.description: The expiration time on or after the generated key will no longer be accepted. This is a time span offset (e.g. "7.00:00:00" for 7 days) that will be added to the current date time to create the expiration date time. [default: 7.00:00:00]
